title: Teaching
text:
I aim to connect statistical theory to real decision-making in my teaching.

Modules at The University of Manchester

MATH4/68011 – Longitudinal and Spatial Data Analysis (2025–present)

MATH4/68011 – Longitudinal Data Analysis (2023–2025)

MATH4/68011 – Linear Models and Nonparametric Regression (2021–2023)

MATH4/68181 – Extreme Values and Financial Risks (2022/23)




Workshops & training

Model-based geostatistics for global public health data (Indonesia, 2025)
Inferential Methods for Random Effects Models (AIMS Rwanda, 2024)
Disease mapping tutorials in R (various workshops incl. UCT, South Africa, 2020)
Model-based geostatistics for spatially aggregated disease data (FUTA, Nigeria, 2019)




Academic service & leadership

PGR Director (Probability & Statistics), Dept. of Mathematics, UoM (07/2023–07/2025)

Departmental Promotions Committee member (2022–2024)

Statistics seminar organiser (2021–2023)




Teaching resources

MBGapp (R/Shiny) — interactive tool for model-based geostatistics:

App: https://olatunjijohnson.shinyapps.io/mbgapp/

Package: https://rdrr.io/github/olatunjijohnson/MBGapp/


If you’re a student looking for supervision in spatial statistics / epidemiology, feel free to get in touch.

title: Disentangling spatial interference and spatial confounding biases in causal inference
text:
Links: arXiv
Summary. Develops methods to separately identify and correct for spatial interference (treatment effects spilling over to neighbours) and spatial confounding (unmeasured spatially structured covariates) in causal inference with geo-referenced data, settings where these two biases commonly co-occur.

title: Spatiotemporal Analysis of COVID-19 Risk in Malawi: The Impact of Age, Poverty, Population Density, and Environmental Factors
text:
Links: medRxiv
Summary. Spatiotemporal modelling of COVID-19 incidence across Malawi, identifying age structure, poverty, population density, and environmental drivers of risk variation across districts and over the course of the pandemic.

title: Accounting for Multiple Covariates in Non-Stationary Geostatistical Modelling
text:
Links: arXiv
Summary. Extends non-stationary geostatistical models to incorporate multiple spatially varying covariates, allowing the spatial covariance structure to change across the study region in a way that is informed by observed environmental and demographic predictors.

title: Geostatistical modelling enables efficient safety assessment for mass drug administration with ivermectin in Loa loa endemic areas through a combined antibody and LoaScope testing strategy for elimination of onchocerciasis
text:
Links: Publisher (DOI) · Open Access (PMC)
Summary. Two-stage strategy (antibody screening → targeted LoaScope) with a multivariate geostatistical model to classify areas as safe/unsafe for ivermectin MDA.

title: MBGapp: A Shiny application for teaching model-based geostatistics to population health scientists
text:
Authors: Olatunji Johnson; Claudio Fronterre; Peter J. Diggle; Benjamin Amoah; Emanuele Giorgi
Journal: PLOS ONE, 16(12):e0262145 (2021)

title: MBGapp: A Shiny application for teaching model-based geostatistics to population health scientists
text:
Summary
Introduces MBGapp, an R/Shiny tool that walks learners through the full MBG workflow (exploration → variogram → model fitting → prediction) using a Loa loa case study, plus a companion VariagramApp for teaching Gaussian processes.

title: MBGapp: A Shiny application for teaching model-based geostatistics to population health scientists
text:
Citation
Johnson O, Fronterre C, Diggle PJ, Amoah B, Giorgi E (2021). MBGapp… PLOS ONE, 16(12):e0262145. https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pone.0262145

title: About me
text:
I’m a Lecturer in Statistics in the Department of Mathematics at The University of Manchester. I was formerly a post-doctoral researcher and PhD student at CHICAS, Lancaster Medical School, Lancaster University (UK). My PhD was supervised by Prof. Peter Diggle, Prof. Emanuele Giorgi and Prof. Jo Knight.
My research develops novel Model-based Geostatistical (MBG) methods to provide valuable answers to many global public health questions and support informed decision-making in low-resource settings. I have worked and currently working on several global public health problems including COPD, malaria and neglected tropical diseases (NTDs) such as river blindness, soil-transmitted helminths, schistosomiasis, Trachoma, Loiasis.
Download CV View Publications Email

title: A spatially discrete approximation to log-Gaussian Cox processes for modelling aggregated disease count data
text:
Links: Publisher (DOI) · Preprint
Summary. Introduces an efficient discrete approximation to LGCPs for aggregated count data, enabling continuous-space prediction (implemented in SDALGCP).

title: Model-Based Geostatistical Methods Enable Efficient Design and Analysis of Prevalence Surveys for Soil-Transmitted Helminth Infection and Other Neglected Tropical Diseases
text:
Authors: Olatunji Johnson; Claudio Fronterre; Benjamin Amoah; Antonio Montresor; Emanuele Giorgi; Nicholas Midzi; Masceline J. Mutsaka-Makuvaza; Ibrahim Kargbo-Labor; Mary H. Hodges; Yaobi Zhang; Collins Okoyo; Charles Mwandawiro; Mark Minnery; Peter J. Diggle
Journal: Clinical Infectious Diseases, 72(Suppl 3):S172–S179 (2021)

title: Model-Based Geostatistical Methods Enable Efficient Design and Analysis of Prevalence Surveys for Soil-Transmitted Helminth Infection and Other Neglected Tropical Diseases
text:
Summary
Shows how MBG-based survey design and analysis can outperform WHO guideline methods, using STH surveys in Kenya, Sierra Leone, and Zimbabwe—achieving improved precision with less field effort.

title: Model-Based Geostatistical Methods Enable Efficient Design and Analysis of Prevalence Surveys for Soil-Transmitted Helminth Infection and Other Neglected Tropical Diseases
text:
Citation
Johnson O, Fronterre C, Amoah B, et al. (2021). Model-Based Geostatistical Methods Enable Efficient Design and Analysis of Prevalence Surveys… Clin Infect Dis, 72(Suppl 3):S172–S179. https://doi.org/10.1093/cid/ciab192

title: Spatial variation and inequities in antenatal care coverage in Kenya, Uganda and mainland Tanzania using model-based geostatistics: a socioeconomic and geographical accessibility lens
text:
Links: Publisher (DOI) · Open Access PDF
Summary. 3×3-km MBG maps reveal ANC4+ inequities across wealth, education and travel-time strata in Kenya, Uganda and mainland Tanzania.

title: Understanding and overcoming geographical barriers for scaling up dog vaccinations against rabies
text:
Links: Publisher (DOI)
Summary. Geospatial analysis of 25 districts in southeastern Tanzania identifies land cover, settlement type, poverty, and elevation as key geographical barriers to achieving high dog vaccination coverage in mass rabies campaigns, and proposes strategies to overcome them.

title: Epidemiological and phylogenetic analyses of public SARS-CoV-2 data from Malawi
text:
Links: Publisher (DOI)
Summary. Integrates epidemiology and phylogenetics to describe five COVID-19 waves in Malawi (2020–2022), highlighting variant dynamics and geographic heterogeneity.

title: Decoupling Distance and Networks: Hybrid Graph Attention-Geostatistical Methods for Spatio-temporal Risk Mapping
text:
Links: arXiv
Summary. Proposes a hybrid framework that combines graph attention networks with model-based geostatistics to capture both network-structured and distance-based spatial dependence in spatio-temporal disease risk mapping, decoupling the two sources of spatial structure.

title: Research
text:
Research interests
My research interests lie in the interface of statistics, epidemiology and health informatics with a particular interest in spatial statistics, public health and environmental epidemiology. I am interested in developing novel model-based geostatistical methods for tropical disease mapping.
My methodological themes include spatial and spatio-temporal statistics; joint modelling of multiple outcomes; geostatistical methods for spatial misalignment and Machine Learning.
My application themes include real-time health surveillance; tropical disease mapping; environmental epidemiology.



PhD Opportunities
I welcome enquiries from motivated students interested in pursuing a PhD in spatial statistics and its applications to global public health. Possible research directions include:

Mixture models for spatially clustered disease data — developing Bayesian mixture approaches to detect and characterise spatial clustering in geo-referenced health surveys, with applications to NTD mapping.
Multivariate spatio-temporal modelling — extending geostatistical frameworks to jointly model multiple correlated outcomes (e.g. co-endemic diseases) across space and time, including scalable inference methods for large datasets.
Hybrid geostatistical and machine learning methods — investigating principled ways to combine the predictive power of machine learning with the uncertainty quantification and interpretability of model-based geostatistics for health risk mapping.
Geostatistical methods for spatially misaligned data — addressing the challenge of combining data collected at incompatible spatial scales (e.g. individual surveys, areal counts, and remote-sensing covariates) within a unified inferential framework.

Prospective students are encouraged to contact me with a CV and a brief statement of their research interests. Funding opportunities (including EPSRC and university scholarships) are available for strong candidates. Please email olatunji.johnson@manchester.ac.uk to discuss.



Current projects

Climate and Health — Causal Inference methods for estimating the indirect effect of climate change on health.
Hybrid Machine learning and Geostatistical models — Investigating how best to combine these approaches.
COVID-19 - Developing spatio-temporal models and mathematical models for COVID-19.




Software

SDALGCP — R package for spatial / spatio-temporal analysis of aggregated disease data https://github.com/olatunjijohnson/SDALGCP

MBGapp — teaching app for model-based geostatistics (Shiny + R package)
App: https://olatunjijohnson.shinyapps.io/mbgapp/ · Package: https://rdrr.io/github/olatunjijohnson/MBGapp/
